prompt: Create an intimate cinematic macro portrait close-up of a young East Asian woman, cropped so only one eye, eyebrow, cheek, part of the nose bridge, and a hint of lips/face edge are visible. The face fills the left and center of the frame, with the right half fading into deep black shadow. Use warm golden-orange directional light from the lower left, creating glossy skin highlights, visible pores, fine eyelashes, a brown iris catchlight, subtle cheek moles, and a quiet emotional mood. Place large out-of-focus orange flower petals in the extreme foreground along the bottom and lower left, partially obscuring the face and creating soft bokeh shapes; the petals should feel very close to the lens. Dark loose hair strands fall across the face and disappear into shadow. Background is almost entirely black, with strong chiaroscuro contrast and a private, hushed atmosphere. Add tiny understated editorial text near the right-center shadow area reading exactly: “petal & breath” on the first line, “no.07” on the second line, and “quiet bloom” on the third line, in a small pale beige serif typeface. Style: high-end beauty photography, fragrance campaign aesthetic, shallow depth of field, macro lens, filmic grain, warm amber highlights, soft shadow detail, square composition, no extra objects, no watermark.
